content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL COMMISSIONS

February 2012

Matched L.E Lewis
P.O. Box 11219
Barackaw CA 92122-1119
Ref:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-12-042

Dear Mr. Lewis:

Reform is a better response to your requests for advice regarding provisions of the Political Reform Act as they relate to the advice under the Act. As you may have addressed many of same sections that are addressed in some of our previous advice letters, common law conflicts of interest.

QUESTION

You have asked whether the fact that you are a business owner in the city affect your ability to campaign for and serve as the city's treasurer?

The Act would not prohibit you from campaigning for or serving as the city treasurer simply because you own a business interests in the city. However, you would be obligated to file any applicable conflict of interest statements pursuant to the Act, and you could face disqualification from certain matters if your business interests create a material financial interest that would be affected by official action.

ANALYSIS

The Act does not prohibit an individual from campaigning for or holding a public office merely because they hold

References to the Act appear parenthetically in this Commission's opinion and are cited to the Government Code Section 81000 through 91014. All references are cited to Government Code Section 81000 et seq.

The Political Reform Act of 1974 is codified in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All references to the Act appear in parenthetically in this Commission's opinion and are cited to Government Code Section 81000 et seq.

File No. A-12-021
Page No. 2


office.² However, the Act may require a public official to disqualify himself or herself from
certain governmental decisions under circumstances that affect their own financial interests. A
public official's private employment could at times cause the official to have a conflict of interest
in certain governmental decisions.

However, until such time as you are holding public office and confronted with a decision
that can affect your business, we cannot advise you with on your specific obligations regarding
the Act's conflict of interest rules. Commission advice in this area is necessarily fact-dependent.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Zackery P. Morazzini
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

JWW:jgl


² Government Code Section 1090 does deal with public official's interests contracts

sections.question:
You have asked whether the fact that you are a business owner in the city affects your 
ability to campaign for and serve as the city's treasurer.
sections.conclusion:
The Act would not prohibit you from campaigning for or serving as the city treasurer due 
to your business interests. However, you would be obligated to disqualify yourself from any city 
treasurer decision that would have a foreseeable· and material financial effect on you or your 
businesses.
sections.analysis:
The Act does not prohibit an individual from campaigning for or holding a public office. 
Nor does the Act prevent a public official from receiving operating a business while holdingrb

office.2 However, the Act may require a public official to disqualify himself or herself from 
certain governmental decisions under circumstances that effect their own financial interests. A 
public official's private employment could at times cause the official to have a conflict of interest 
in certain governmental decisions. 
However, until such time as you are holding public office and confronted with a decision 
that can affect your business, we cannot advise you with on your specific obligations regarding 
the Act's conflict of interest rules. Commission advice in this area is necessarily fact-dependent.

QUESTION: You have asked whether the fact that you are a business owner in the city affects your 
ability to campaign for and serve as the city's treasurer.

CONCLUSION: The Act would not prohibit you from campaigning for or serving as the city treasurer due 
to your business interests. However, you would be obligated to disqualify yourself from any city 
treasurer decision that would have a foreseeable· and material financial effect on you or your 
businesses.
